success_surface: Character-level cell value transformation while preserving format specificity
effective_behaviors: Selected openpyxl over pandas for direct cell-level manipulation requiring formatting preservation
Implemented type-aware data transformation handling None, numeric, and string inputs separately
Defined positive character whitelist (what to keep) rather than negative filter (what to remove)
Normalized edge cases like multiple decimal points through explicit parsing logic
Scoped operations to exact target range without unintended side effects
Validated transformation results before saving modified workbook
evidence: Used load_workbook() from openpyxl instead of pandas.read_excel()
Created clean_numeric() function with separate branches for None, int/float, and string types
Filtering logic explicitly preserves both c.isdigit() AND c == '.' characters
Handled multiple decimal points with split().join() normalization keeping last decimal point
Iterated through columns 1-9 for exact range A1:H1 without affecting other cells
Printed confirmation message after successful save operation

memory_items.title: Tool Selection for Cell-Level Operations
memory_items.lesson: Use openpyxl for direct cell manipulation when formatting, formulas, or granular control matters more than bulk processing speed.
memory_items.generalization: Prefer openpyxl over pandas when task requires cell-by-cell transformations, formatting preservation, or avoiding automatic formula-to-value conversion.

memory_items.title: Type-Safe Value Transformation
memory_items.lesson: Handle input data heterogeneity by detecting and processing different value types (None, numeric, string) with dedicated branches.
memory_items.generalization: Always check isinstance(value, types) before applying string operations; provide fallback paths for None/null values.

memory_items.title: Positive Character Filtering Strategy
memory_items.lesson: When cleaning text, define explicitly which characters to KEEP rather than which to REMOVE for more predictable results.
memory_items.generalization: Use whitelisting approach (c in allowed_set) for selective character retention tasks like sanitizing numbers, currencies, or codes.

memory_items.title: Decimal Point Normalization
memory_items.lesson: Multiple decimal points in cleaned strings require post-processing to retain exactly one valid decimal separator.
memory_items.generalization: After character filtering, validate number format by removing excess special characters while preserving essential ones at correct positions.

memory_items.title: Precision Range Targeting
memory_items.lesson: Explicitly iterate over required rows/columns using numeric indices to ensure exact scope compliance.
memory_items.generalization: Use ws.cell(row=X, column=Y) or named ranges to confine modifications to specified area without affecting adjacent data.

edits.content:
## Cell-Level Transformation Patterns

### Type-Aware Data Cleaning
- Check data type before transformation: handle None, numeric types (int/float), and strings with separate logic paths
- Convert numeric types to string representation first if character-level filtering is needed

### Positive Character Whitelisting
- Define explicitly which characters to RETAIN (e.g., digits, decimal points) rather than what to discard
- Use conditional logic: `if char in allowed_set` instead of regex negation for critical formatting characters

### Special Character Normalization
- Post-process filtered strings to normalize special characters (e.g., ensure single decimal point in numbers)
- Split/join strategy for multi-occurrence markers: keep final occurrence for proper numeric format

### Direct Workbook Access Pattern
- For granular cell edits preserving original structure: load with openpyxl → modify individual cells via .value → save
- Avoid pandas intermediate conversion when per-cell inspection/modification is required

### Range Confinement Protocol
- Use explicit loop bounds matching instruction scope (row X to Y, column A to H)
- Validate iteration coverage matches specification before writing changes
